steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Indigenous words 'came to English indirectly from' their source languages and 'entered English through Spanish.' The correct choice must accurately describe Spanish's role as a conduit — not as an equal partner, a numerical balance, or a mutual borrower.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It has served as a medium through which Indigenous languages have influenced English.: matches 'came to English through Spanish' exactly; Spanish is the intermediary bridge between Indigenous languages and English. ✓
  • Choice B — Its contribution to English vocabulary roughly equals the collective contribution by Indigenous languages.: implies a parallel relationship the passage never establishes; Spanish is a passthrough, not a co-contributor. ✗
  • Choice C — It adopted Nahuatl and Quechua words in approximately equal numbers.: introduces a specific count or proportion that the passage does not mention. ✗
  • Choice D — It has borrowed words from Indigenous languages and contributed words to them.: suggests Indigenous languages also borrowed from Spanish, reversing the direction of influence described in the passage. ✗
That is the correct answer.
